# Verify that git diff shows plaintext diffs for sesam-tracked secrets.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'
exec git add .gitkeep
exec git commit -m 'initial'

# Init sesam: creates README.md, registers it as a secret, seals it.
exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Modify the secret and re-seal. Seal also wires up the git textconv config.
cp modified_readme.md README.md
exec sesam seal

# Sanity-check: git sees the encrypted object as modified, textconv is configured.
exec git status --short
stdout '.sesam/objects/README.md.sesam'
exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff.textconv
stdout 'sesam show'
exec git check-attr diff -- .sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
stdout 'sesam-diff'

# Without textconv, git treats the file as binary.
exec git diff HEAD --no-textconv -- .sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
stdout 'Binary files'

# git diff HEAD decrypts both versions and shows a plaintext diff.
exec git diff HEAD -- .sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
stdout 'sesam bootstrap'
stdout 'modified readme'

-- .gitkeep --
-- modified_readme.md --
# modified readme

This is the new content after the change.
